comment_text.sn38.11:1.1: The Buddha lived and taught in the Gangetic plain, a region characterized by deadly and unpredictable flooding.

root_text.sn38.11:1.6: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etesaṁ oghānaṁ pahānāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.11:1.7: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etesaṁ oghānaṁ pahānāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.11:1.8: “Katamo panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā, etesaṁ oghānaṁ pahānāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.11:1.9: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo, etesaṁ oghānaṁ pahānāya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.11:1.11: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā, etesaṁ oghānaṁ pahānāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.11:1.12: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā, etesaṁ oghānaṁ pahānāya.

translation_text.sn38.11:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘a flood’.
translation_text.sn38.11:1.4: The floods of sensuality, desire to be reborn, views, and ignorance.
translation_text.sn38.11:1.6: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for completely understanding these floods?”
